title: A Dinosaur with a Head Spike Found in the Desert
summary: Scientists found a new dinosaur called Spinosaurus in the Sahara Desert. This dinosaur had a big spike on its head and ate fish. Before, people thought Spinosaurus could dive deep in the ocean like in movies, but the new fossils show it lived near rivers far from the sea. The scientists, led by Paul Sereno, discovered the bones in Niger with help from local guides. They think Spinosaurus was too buoyant to dive because its bones had air sacks, like birds. Instead, it probably waded in shallow water to catch fish, similar to a heron. This finding changes what we know about how Spinosaurus lived and hunted.

a big, hot desert in Africa where the dinosaur was found

questions.question: Where was the new Spinosaurus found?
Who led the team that found the fossils?
How did Spinosaurus likely catch fish?
What special feature did the new Spinosaurus have on its head?

background_read: Spinosaurus was a dinosaur that lived long ago during the time of other famous dinosaurs like T. rex. Scientists have found its fossils in different places, and movies like Jurassic Park show it in different ways. Some thought it could swim and dive, but new discoveries help us understand it better. Fossils are important because they tell us about animals from the past, and scientists use them to learn how dinosaurs lived and what they looked like.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists led by Paul Sereno found a new Spinosaurus dinosaur.
WHAT: The dinosaur had a head spike and lived near rivers, not in the ocean, and it waded to catch fish instead of diving.
WHERE: The fossils were discovered in the Sahara Desert in Niger.
WHY: This finding helps correct earlier ideas about Spinosaurus and shows how it really lived.

perspectives.description: New fossils change what we know about dinosaur behavior.
